_comment: H-3-CLOSURE-DESIGN.md §5 (HC-2): context anchors, prefix blocklists AND prefix whitelists for the regex identifier patterns that have no check digit of their own (or whose check digit can legitimately fail on a scanned/re-typed number). Closed lists, same discipline as surname-gazetteer.json / special-category-lexicon.json — extend only via a newly measured leak + test, never speculatively. `contextAnchors` are plain regex source strings (no flags baked in; anonymizer.js compiles them with 'iu') tested for PRESENCE anywhere in a bounded backward window from the candidate match — NOT anchored to the character immediately before the candidate — and the window itself is truncated at a paragraph break (\n\n) so context never leaks across an unrelated paragraph. `contextWindow` is the window length in characters, per pattern (§5.2-§5.4). `countyPrefixes` (O-HC-1, added 2026-07-19) is a different kind of gate on the same shape: instead of requiring lexical context nearby, it requires the matched prefix itself to be a real, closed-list value — see the field's own comment under `tablicaRejestracyjna` for source/methodology.

dowodOsobisty._comment: R-DOW §5.2. blocklistPrefixes (O-HC-3): acronyms that are also valid-shaped 3-letter series prefixes and must never be emitted via the arithmetic path (A) even if the 9-char token happens to pass the check-digit formula by chance (~1/10 of random 3L+6D tokens do, R-HC-3) — defense in depth alongside the arithmetic itself. contextAnchors (path B): literal mentions of the document that let a checksum-invalid (e.g. OCR-damaged) number through anyway.

prawoJazdy._comment: R-PJ §5.3. WYŁĄCZNIE kotwiczone, nigdy bare: numeracja faktur/dokumentów księgowych ("Faktura VAT nr 12345/07/2024") ma identyczny kształt 5/2/4 — bez anchor ten wzorzec maskowałby numery faktur jako PERSON_IDENTIFIER w każdym piśmie gospodarczym.

tablicaRejestracyjna._comment: R-TR §5.4. Anchored path (unchanged): 2-3 uppercase letters + 4-5 alphanumerics (≥1 digit) collides head-on with currency amounts ("CHF 250 000", "USD 88812", "PLN 12345") — an unconditional bare pattern would mask amounts as VEHICLE_IDENTIFIER in every credit-agreement fragment (the exact corpus this tool is built for), so this path still requires a context anchor. O-HC-1 (decyzja Alana 2026-07-19): ADDED a second, bare path guarded by `countyPrefixes` instead of an anchor — see the comment on that field for the full contract.
tablicaRejestracyjna._countyPrefixesComment: O-HC-1: real Polish vehicle-plate prefixes (wyróżnik województwa + wyróżnik powiatu), used to gate the BARE (no-context-anchor) R-TR variant — a candidate only emits without an anchor nearby if its 2-3 letter prefix is a REAL county/city code, which is what makes it safe against currency amounts written ISO-code-first (see the property test in identifier-patterns.test.js: this list is proven disjoint from src/pipeline/data/iso-4217-currency-codes.json). Source: Załącznik nr 13 'WYRÓŻNIKI WOJEWÓDZTW I POWIATÓW DLA TABLIC REJESTRACYJNYCH' to rozporządzenia Ministra Infrastruktury z dnia 8 listopada 2024 r. w sprawie rejestracji i oznaczania pojazdów, wymagań dla tablic rejestracyjnych oraz wzorów innych dokumentów związanych z rejestracją pojazdów — fetched 2026-07-19 via the PAP Samorząd mirror (samorzad.pap.pl/sites/default/files/2024-01/wyróżniki-powiaty.pdf; NOT the primary Dziennik Ustaw/ISAP text — recommend a primary-source cross-check before treating this as litigation-grade authoritative, same caveat as any secondary-source legal citation). Believed COMPLETE for all 16 voivodeships' regular/zabytkowe/dyplomatyczne plate prefixes (annex columns 4x5): every row was transcribed by hand into a small script (cartesian product of voivodeship letter(s) x county/city code(s) computed in code, not by hand, to avoid manual combination errors across ~380 rows) and the resulting unit count (380) matches Poland's well-known total of 314 powiaty + 66 miasta na prawach powiatu — a strong structural cross-check that no row was dropped or duplicated, but this is still a single-pass transcription that has not been independently double-keyed, so a second review before relying on it for anything beyond this defensive whitelist is recommended. The annex's column 6 (temporary/individual plates, e.g. "D0-D9" — voivodeship letter + DIGIT) is a structurally different, disjoint shape from this LLL/LL prefix list and is deliberately excluded: TR_CANDIDATE_RE's prefix group is letters-only, so a letter+digit temporary-plate code could never match it anyway.
tablicaRejestracyjna._countyPrefixesCurrencyExclusionsComment: 7 prefixes that the cartesian product above legitimately produces as REAL county/city codes, but which are EXCLUDED from `countyPrefixes` (defense in depth, same discipline as R-DOW's O-HC-3 `blocklistPrefixes`) because they collide with an ISO-4217 currency code — discovered BY running the property test against the raw 677-entry derivation, not assumed. AOR=Mazowieckie(A)+ostrowski, ARA=Mazowieckie(A)+radomski, GWE=Pomorskie(G)+wejherowski, LKR=Lubelskie(L)+kraśnicki, MNT=Wielkopolskie(M)+nowotomyski, PKR=Wielkopolskie(P)+krotoszyński, TOP=Świętokrzyskie(T)+opatowski. Each is a real plate prefix for that county, but a bare match on e.g. 'PKR 12345' would be indistinguishable from a Pakistani-rupee amount, so it keeps working ONLY through the existing anchored path (unaffected) and never through the bare path.

paszport._comment: R-PASZ §8 O-HC-2. WYŁĄCZNIE kotwiczone, bez sumy kontrolnej w v1 (nie zmyślamy algorytmu paszportu — dopiero po potwierdzeniu). 2 wielkie litery + 7 cyfr jest tym samym 9-znakowym budżetem co R-DOW (3 litery + 6 cyfr), ale rozłączne strukturalnie: dowolny prefiks kończący się nie-cyfrą/nie-'O' łamie próbę odczytania 2L+7C wewnątrz 3L+6D, a odwrotnie trzecia litera R-DOW blokuje domknięcie 7-cyfrowego okna R-PASZ, chyba że tą literą jest akurat 'O' (rzadki, nieszkodliwy zbieg: obie interpretacje wskazują ten sam span i typ PERSON_IDENTIFIER, więc dedup i tak scala je w jedną encję).
